description: Conversion-funnel events (view content -> ATC -> checkout -> purchase) and business-value metrics (AOV, LTV, CAC, MER, payback). These connect ad efficiency to actual profitability.

landing_page_view.name: Landing Page View (LPV)
landing_page_view.definition: Counted when a person clicks a link and the destination page actually finishes loading — stricter than a link click.
landing_page_view.meaning: Reveals click-to-load drop-off. A large gap between link clicks and LPVs exposes a slow or broken page.
landing_page_view.critical: Ratio below 60% (severe page-speed or redirect problem).
best_practices: Use LPV (not link clicks) as the CVR denominator for a truer post-click read

view_content.name: View Content / Product Detail Page View
view_content.definition: Event fired when a person views a product or content detail page. Motion: indicates shopping consideration.
view_content.meaning: Top-of-shopping-funnel intent signal; the base of the product-page-to-purchase funnel.

benchmark.note: Evaluate as funnel ratios (VC->ATC->IC->Purchase), not in isolation.

view_content.warning: Healthy traffic but low ViewContent (users not reaching product pages).
view_content.critical: ViewContent firing but ATC near zero (product/price/page problem).
possible_causes: Landing on generic pages not product pages
recommendations: Send traffic to specific product pages
Verify the ViewContent event fires correctly
best_practices: Map the full event funnel and watch step-to-step conversion
common_mistakes: Optimising for ViewContent (cheap, low-intent) instead of purchases

add_to_cart.name: Add To Cart (ATC)
add_to_cart.definition: Event fired when a product is added to the cart. Motion: signals serious purchase consideration.
add_to_cart.meaning: Mid-funnel intent. The ViewContent->ATC ratio gauges product/price appeal; ATC->Purchase gauges checkout health.

benchmark.note: Varies by vertical and price point.

add_to_cart.warning: ATC strong but checkout-initiated weak (cart abandonment).
add_to_cart.critical: ViewContent high, ATC near zero (price or product mismatch).
best_practices: Build retargeting audiences from ATC events
Use ATC as a secondary optimisation event when purchases are sparse
common_mistakes: Optimising on ATC long-term instead of graduating to purchase
notes_edge_cases: ATC can be inflated by wishlist behaviour; validate against purchases.

checkout_initiated.name: Initiate Checkout (IC)
checkout_initiated.definition: Event fired when a user begins the checkout flow (entering payment/shipping). Motion: a stronger intent signal than add-to-cart.
checkout_initiated.meaning: Late-funnel, high-intent signal. The IC->Purchase ratio isolates payment/checkout friction.
common_mistakes: Ignoring IC->Purchase leakage and blaming the ad

purchase.name: Purchase
purchase.definition: Completion of a transaction. Motion: the primary optimisation target for performance advertisers.
purchase.meaning: The terminal value event for commerce. Drives CPA, ROAS, and revenue.

benchmark.note: Volume gate for exiting Learning Phase: ~50 optimisation events per ad set per week.

purchase.warning: Fewer than ~50 purchases/ad set/week (Learning Limited risk).
purchase.critical: Purchases collapsing while upper funnel (VC/ATC/IC) holds (checkout break or tracking loss).
recommendations: Consolidate ad sets to reach the 50-event threshold
best_practices: Optimise for purchase once volume allows; otherwise use a reliable upper event
Deploy CAPI to recover browser-lost conversions
common_mistakes: Splitting budget so thin no ad set exits learning
notes_edge_cases: Reported purchases depend on the attribution window; default 7-day click / 1-day view.

purchase_roas.name: Purchase ROAS (Meta-attributed)
purchase_roas.definition: Meta's own purchase_roas field — total purchase value attributed to the ad divided by spend, keyed by purchase action type.
purchase_roas.meaning: Platform-reported ROAS used when present; preferred over computed value/spend because it reflects Meta's attribution.
purchase_roas.warning: Diverging materially below blended MER (attribution over- or under-count).
recommendations: Prefer a single canonical purchase type (e.g. omni_purchase) — do not sum overlapping types
best_practices: When multiple purchase_roas entries exist, pick by preference order, never sum (double-counts)
notes_edge_cases: Adlytic's insightMapper prefers omni_purchase, then purchase, then pixel purchase — and never sums them.

AOV.name: Average Order Value
AOV.definition: Mean revenue per transaction. Motion: influences the acceptable CAC threshold.
AOV.meaning: Sets the ceiling on sustainable acquisition cost and directly scales ROAS at fixed CVR.

benchmark.note: Business-specific; track trend and segment by audience/creative.

AOV.warning: AOV declining (discount dependence or wrong-product traffic).
AOV.critical: AOV below the value needed to clear CPA at target margin.
best_practices: Raise AOV to improve ROAS without touching ad cost
common_mistakes: Ignoring AOV when setting CPA targets

LTV.name: Customer Lifetime Value
LTV.definition: Total revenue (or profit) a customer generates over their entire relationship. Meta/Audience Network frames it as forecast profit per average user.
LTV.formula: AOV * Purchase Frequency * Customer Lifespan (or cohort-summed margin)
LTV.meaning: Higher LTV permits a higher allowable CAC. The strategic lever that lets you outbid competitors on acquisition.
LTV.critical: LTV:CAC below 1:1 (unprofitable acquisition even over lifetime).
possible_causes: One-time-buyer product with no repeat motion
recommendations: Set max CAC from LTV, not first-order AOV
best_practices: Budget acquisition against LTV where retention data is reliable
Use cohort LTV, not a single blended average
common_mistakes: Capping CAC at first-order value when repeat revenue is strong
notes_edge_cases: Forecast LTV is a model; treat early-cohort LTV as provisional.

CAC.name: Customer Acquisition Cost
CAC.definition: The fully-loaded average cost of sales and marketing to acquire one NEW customer. Deeper than CPA because it counts only net-new customers and may include non-ad costs.
CAC.formula: Total Sales & Marketing Cost / New Customers Acquired
CAC.meaning: The true acquisition economics. Unlike CPA (per conversion), CAC isolates new-customer cost and pairs with LTV.
CAC.critical: CAC above contribution margin per customer.
recommendations: Separate new vs returning in reporting
Lower CPA via the funnel diagnosis
Shift budget to channels with lower true CAC
best_practices: Require first-party data to distinguish new customers
Always evaluate CAC against LTV, never alone
common_mistakes: Counting repeat purchases as new acquisitions
notes_edge_cases: CAC may include creative, agency, and tooling costs beyond media spend.

MER.name: Marketing Efficiency Ratio
MER.definition: Total revenue divided by total marketing spend across ALL channels. Motion: blended ROAS.
MER.meaning: Attribution-proof top-line efficiency. Cross-checks platform ROAS, which post-iOS14 understates true contribution.

benchmark.note: Set per business from margin; compare MER trend to platform ROAS trend.

MER.warning: MER falling while platform ROAS looks stable (attribution masking real decline).
MER.critical: MER below break-even across the account.
recommendations: Use MER as the source of truth for scaling decisions
Triangulate platform ROAS, MER, and incrementality
best_practices: Steer total budget by MER; use platform ROAS for relative creative/audience comparisons
common_mistakes: Trusting summed platform ROAS as if it were incremental
notes_edge_cases: MER is blended — it cannot attribute, only bound, channel performance.

cart_abandonment_rate.name: Cart Abandonment Rate
cart_abandonment_rate.definition: Share of carts (or checkouts) started but not completed.
cart_abandonment_rate.meaning: Quantifies late-funnel leakage and the size of the retargetable recovery audience.
best_practices: Build the highest-intent retargeting audience from abandoners
common_mistakes: Treating high abandonment as inevitable rather than recoverable
notes_edge_cases: Some abandonment is normal comparison shopping; focus on the recoverable margin.

cost_per_lead.name: Cost Per Lead (CPL)
cost_per_lead.definition: Average spend to capture one lead (form submission / contact).
cost_per_lead.meaning: Primary efficiency metric for lead-gen objectives where there is no immediate order value.
cost_per_lead.warning: CPL above the vertical range with no lead-quality offset.
cost_per_lead.critical: CPL above the value of a qualified lead (lead value = close rate * deal value).
recommendations: Pre-qualify in the ad to raise lead quality
best_practices: Track cost per QUALIFIED lead, not raw CPL
Feed CRM outcomes back via offline conversions
common_mistakes: Optimising raw CPL and flooding sales with junk leads
notes_edge_cases: Instant-form leads are cheaper but often lower intent than landing-page leads.

retention_rate.name: Retention Rate
retention_rate.definition: Percentage of acquired users/customers who return and continue to use the product or buy again. Meta: share of installers who returned.
retention_rate.meaning: Drives LTV and therefore allowable CAC. Retention, not acquisition, usually decides long-run profitability.

benchmark.note: Cohort- and category-specific; track Day-1/7/30 (apps) or repeat-within-90d (commerce).

recommendations: Acquire audiences that resemble high-retention cohorts
best_practices: Optimise acquisition toward audiences with the best downstream retention, not the cheapest CPA
common_mistakes: Scaling acquisition that brings poorly-retaining users
